original_hypothesis: SLC can be optimized for inference to improve hit rate and reduce DRAM traffic.
evidence_against: Main 30 A3 measured 80-160 GB/s sustained read bandwidth from the SLC controller (AMCC) during 8B ANE inference, confirming that the SLC acts as a mandatory transit layer for DRAM reads. The working set (8B Q8 = 7.2 GB, 70B Q4 = 37 GB) is much larger than SLC capacity (~48 MB), so each weight read is a cache miss that goes to DRAM.
scope: SLC is dead as a hit-rate optimization for inference. The working set is too large to fit in SLC, and there is no temporal reuse opportunity.
remaining_leads: is the AMCC controller's bandwidth a real bottleneck?

path_name: SLC cache hints for any production workload
original_hypothesis: SLC cache hints (e.g., encodeCacheHintTag) can improve performance for production workloads.
evidence_against: Main 30 A3 confirmed that the SLC acts as a mandatory transit layer for DRAM reads during inference, with 80-160 GB/s sustained read bandwidth. The working set is much larger than SLC capacity, so each weight read is a cache miss that goes to DRAM.
scope: SLC cache hints are dead for hit-rate optimization. encodeCacheHintTag pins for hit-rate, which doesn't apply to inference workloads with working sets much larger than SLC capacity.
